title: Feat/add force capability on export
body:
##  Summary

<!--
Provide a concise summary of what this pull request is addressing.

If this PR fixes any issues, list them here by number (e.g., Fixes #123).
-->
This pull request introduces a --force / -f flag to the marimo export subcommands. This allows users to overwrite existing output files, which is essential for automation and scripting use cases.

Fixes #5523

##  Description of Changes

<!--
Detail the specific changes made in this pull request. Explain the problem addressed and how it was resolved. If applicable, provide before and after comparisons, screenshots, or any relevant details to help reviewers understand the changes easily.
-->

Currently, when marimo export is used with an --output argument that points to an existing file, it interactively prompts the user for confirmation before overwriting. While this is a safe default for manual use, it blocks non-interactive workflows, such as in CI/CD pipelines or automated scripts.

This PR resolves the issue by:

Adding a --force and -f option to all relevant export commands (html, script, md, ipynb, and html_wasm).

Updating the watch_and_export function to check for this flag. If force is True, the prompt_to_overwrite confirmation step is skipped, and the file is overwritten directly.

Adding a new pytest test case (test_cli_export_html_force_overwrite) to ensure the new functionality works as expected. The test verifies that:

Attempting to export to an existing file without the flag does not overwrite it.

Using --force successfully overwrites the existing file.

Using the short alias -f also works correctly.

These changes make the export feature more versatile and friendly for automated environments without altering the safe default behavior for interactive users.

I know it is not perfect, please @dmadisetti @mscolnick check this one out!
##  Checklist

- [x] I have read the [contributor guidelines](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [x] For large changes, or changes that affect the public API: this change was discussed or approved through an issue, on [Discord](https://marimo.io/discord?ref=pr), or the community [discussions](https://github.com/marimo-team/marimo/discussions) (Please provide a link if applicable).
- [x] I have added tests for the changes made.
- [x] I have run the code and verified that it works as expected.

step_plan.summary: Add a --force / -f flag to all marimo export subcommands (html, script, md, ipynb, html_wasm) and update the shared watch_and_export function to skip the interactive overwrite prompt when force=True, enabling non-interactive overwrites for automation while preserving the safe default behavior.

steps.operation: Add force parameter and skip interactive overwrite confirmation
steps.reason: Centralize non-interactive overwrite logic: add force: bool = False to the watch_and_export signature and, when an output path is provided, skip calling prompt_to_overwrite(output_path) if force is True so exports can overwrite files without user interaction. This keeps the existing safe default (prompting) when force is False and ensures a single codepath controls overwrite behavior for all export subcommands.
steps.side_effects: Changes the internal function signature — any other internal callers of watch_and_export must be updated to accept the new parameter (likely only the CLI subcommands in the same module). Tests that rely on prompt_to_overwrite behavior (especially in non-tty CI) may need to be adjusted; add a new pytest that asserts force overwrites. No external public API change for users other than the CLI flags added in subsequent steps. Ensure type hints and call sites updated to avoid breakage.

steps.operation: Add --force / -f option to html subcommand and pass through to watch_and_export
steps.reason: Expose the force flag to the HTML export CLI so users can overwrite html output non-interactively; add click.option('--force', '-f', is_flag=True, default=False, help='Overwrite existing output files without prompting (useful for automation).') and pass force into watch_and_export(..., force=force).
steps.side_effects: Modifies CLI help text and behavior for html export only when flag provided (backwards compatible). Tests should validate both default (no overwrite) and --force behavior. If other code calls html() programmatically, it will accept the new parameter implicitly via click, but internal calls to watch_and_export must pass the new argument. Might require updating any documentation or example scripts that assume prompting always occurs.

steps.operation: Add --force / -f option to script subcommand and pass through to watch_and_export
steps.reason: Enable non-interactive overwrites for script exports by adding the same --force / -f click option and forwarding the boolean to watch_and_export so existing script files can be overwritten in automation scenarios.
steps.side_effects: Changes CLI behavior/help for the script export command (safe default unchanged). Ensure the subcommand invocation now includes the force argument when calling watch_and_export; otherwise static analysis or tests may fail. Affects CI and automated uses of the script export command and may require test updates.

steps.operation: Add --force / -f option to md subcommand and pass through to watch_and_export
steps.reason: Allow Markdown exports to be overwritten non-interactively by adding the --force / -f option and passing it to watch_and_export. This keeps behavior consistent across export formats.
steps.side_effects: Updates CLI help for md export. Tests that verify overwrite prompting must be mindful of GLOBAL_SETTINGS and tty detection; add/adjust tests accordingly. If any downstream tooling depends on interactive prompt behavior, it remains unchanged unless --force is used.

steps.operation: Add --force / -f option to ipynb subcommand and pass through to watch_and_export
steps.reason: Support non-interactive overwriting of .ipynb export files by adding the --force / -f click option and forwarding it into watch_and_export (ensure that sandbox/include-outputs path flow is preserved while only altering the overwrite confirmation step).
steps.side_effects: CLI help and usage change for ipynb exports. Ensure that ipynb-specific logic (e.g., include-outputs handling) still runs unchanged and only the overwrite prompt is skipped when force is True. Tests for ipynb export may need to be added/updated in future to cover this flag.

steps.operation: Add --force / -f option to html_wasm subcommand and pass through to watch_and_export
steps.reason: Enable non-interactive overwrites for the WASM HTML export path: add the --force / -f click option and ensure the prepared asset outfile passed into watch_and_export receives the force value so the prompt is skipped and files are overwritten in automation contexts.
steps.side_effects: Changes CLI help for html_wasm. Because html_wasm prepares assets before calling watch_and_export, ensure the assets generation still occurs; only the overwrite confirmation is skipped when force True. Tests must be added/updated (see related test addition) to cover that exporting to an existing file is not overwritten by default but is overwritten when using --force or -f. Also check for any callers that assumed watch_and_export signature unchanged.
